description_long: This object is a firearm with a classic design, featuring a long, cylindrical barrel and a wooden full stock. The receiver and barrel are constructed from dark metal, with the barrel extending forward and a wooden fore-end grip beneath it. The trigger is housed within a metal trigger guard, and the magazine well is positioned in front of the trigger, suggesting a box magazine feed. The rear of the firearm has a robust wooden stock, ergonomically shaped for shoulder support. The overall appearance is reminiscent of early to mid-20th century submachine guns, with a blend of wood and metal materials and a utilitarian, rugged finish.
description: A classic firearm with a long metal barrel, wooden full stock, and a box magazine well, blending wood and metal in a rugged, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the firearm, highlighting the wooden stock, fore-end grip, and the box magazine well.
description_view_1: Front-on view displaying the narrow profile of the barrel and fore-end, with minimal detail visible except the muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the wooden stock, fore-end, and metal receiver.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel, with the trigger guard and magazine well barely visible.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, standard barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metal guard with a smooth contour and no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
